CREATE TABLE test_table_1 (id INTEGER, value INTEGER);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table_1 WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE test_table_2 (id INTEGER, category VARCHAR);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table_1 JOIN test_table_2 ON test_table_1.id = test_table_2.id;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT id FROM test_table_1) AS unique_ids;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id, COUNT(value) AS value_count FROM test_table_1 GROUP BY id) AS grouped_values WHERE value_count > 1;
SELECT category, COUNT(*) FROM test_table_2 GROUP BY category;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM test_table_1 WHERE value > 15) AS filtered;
SELECT COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY category) FROM test_table_2;
SELECT A.id, COUNT(B.value) FROM test_table_1 A LEFT JOIN test_table_2 B ON A.id = B.id GROUP BY A.id;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT * FROM test_table_1 WHERE value < 0) AS negative_values;
